mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive equity returns. This is a persistent, always-on signal of underlying economic strength.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises significantly above its 12-month moving average, it signals a deterioration in the labor market that precedes broader economic weakness and lower corporate profits, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is raising the FEDFUNDS rate while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also accelerating, the combined effect creates a highly restrictive environment for financial assets, compressing valuation multiples and leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on signal triggers fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals efficient supply-side growth and improving corporate profitability without stoking fears of monetary tightening, thus driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism's predictive power (measured by Rank IC) is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during periods where CPI YoY is below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) provides a high-frequency indicator of consumer strength, which translates directly into positive revenue surprises and improved short-term investor sentiment.
failure_condition: The correlation between the signal's value and 5-day forward returns is not statistically different from zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the FEDFUNDS rate is exceptionally low (below 0.5%), the resulting low cost of capital and accommodative financial conditions provide a powerful tailwind for equity valuations, leading to positive returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% are not statistically higher than when it is above 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a slowdown in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, signaling falling aggregate demand and triggering negative forward returns for the broader market.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict more than 50% of NBER-dated recessions with a 6-month lead time, or its hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a classic indicator of an impending recession, leading to a broad-based sell-off in equities.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < prior quarter growth AND UNRATE > 6-month moving average
failure_condition: The combined signal has a false positive rate for predicting negative 60-day returns that exceeds 50% over any 10-year period.
